limelight-frolic-vaporizer.summary: A CNC-machined hybrid portable for patients who want serious output and a fully isolated airpath.
limelight-frolic-vaporizer.description: The Frolic pairs a stainless-steel hybrid heater with a fully isolated PEEK airpath, so convection lifts the terpenes cleanly while gentle conduction from the chamber walls finishes the extraction evenly. It runs two styles: Session Mode (40–220°C) for set-and-draw, and an on-demand mode up to 240°C, controlled through a colour OLED display. Power comes from a removable 21700 5000mAh cell, so you swap rather than wait once it runs flat. The modular, serviceable build suits patients who medicate heavily and want a device they can keep running for years. At this price it sits at the top end, so it makes most sense if you vape daily and value airpath purity and repairability.

norddampf-hammah.summary: A pocket-sized hybrid vaporiser with glass aroma pods, built for simple one-button dosing.
norddampf-hammah.description: The HAMMAH is a compact hybrid (convection plus conduction) device using Norddampf's GlassPod system, with short and long borosilicate pods that keep the vapour path glass for cleaner flavour. It heats in around 30 seconds, runs five presets from 180–220°C, and gets 6–10 sessions from the 1,600mAh battery with USB-C fast charging. At 90mm and 85g it genuinely pockets, and one-button operation keeps it straightforward for daily medical use. The internal battery means you charge rather than swap cells, so it suits a patient who medicates a few times a day rather than all day away from power.

norddampf-relict.summary: A dual-heating conduction vaporiser with airflow-sensing heat boost, for patients who want dense draws on demand.
norddampf-relict.description: The Relict heats the chamber from both the floor and sides for even conduction, and its Intellisense airflow sensors detect your inhale and boost the heat for denser vapour. Temperature is precise from 160–240°C in 1°C steps, set on an OLED display that rotates for handheld or water-piece use, with three-button control and vibration feedback. The 0.3g stainless steel dosing capsules keep loading tidy on the go, and a 45mm glass aroma tube cools the vapour. A 2600mAh battery and sub-30-second heat-up make it ready quickly, suiting a patient who wants control and a bit of power in a compact 138g body.

norddampf-voity.summary: An affordable hybrid vaporiser with a ceramic chamber, for first-time or mid-tier dry-herb patients.
norddampf-voity.description: The Voity combines conduction, convection and infrared heating in a nano-glazed ceramic C-Core chamber that wipes clean and keeps herb from sticking. Three presets (180, 200, 220°C) keep operation simple, and a magnetic zirconia mouthpiece protects flavour. It heats in about 30 seconds and gets up to 12 sessions from the 1,800mAh battery, charged over USB-C in around an hour. At 85g and a low price it's a sensible entry point for a patient who wants real hybrid heating without spending much. Fixed presets mean less fine control than dialled devices, which is the trade-off for the simplicity.

pax-flow.summary: A discreet hybrid pocket vaporiser from PAX with five heat settings and a four-year warranty.
pax-flow.description: The PAX Flow uses hybrid convection and conduction heating in the slim, recognisable PAX form, ready to use in around 30 seconds. Five heat settings let you tune flavour against vapour density, and two mouthpieces (flat and raised) plus a maintenance kit come in the box. USB-C charging and a 98mm body make it easy to carry and top up. It suits a patient who values discretion and a clean, simple device over deep customisation, backed by a four-year warranty. Session battery life is on the shorter side, so it's better for portable top-ups than long sessions away from power.

pax-mini.summary: A compact one-button conduction vaporiser from PAX, for patients who want simple, reliable dosing.
pax-mini.description: The PAX Mini 2 strips things back to one-button operation with four intelligent heat modes that adjust automatically through the session, so there's nothing to fiddle with. Conduction heating gets it ready in about 22 seconds, and half-pack and full-pack oven lids let you match the load to how much you need. Charging is via a magnetic USB-C cable, with a 2-year warranty. It holds up to 0.25g and suits a patient who wants a pocketable, low-maintenance device without presets or apps to learn. Conduction means it's best loaded and used promptly rather than left part-finished.

puffco-proxy.summary: A modular concentrate and hash vaporiser from Puffco that attaches to different glass, for patients prescribed extracts.
puffco-proxy.description: The Puffco Proxy is a one-button device for concentrates and hash, built around a 3D Chamber and a detachable base that fits the included glass pipe or other Proxy glassware. It heats in about 30 seconds, offers a boost mode and 15 heat settings, and gives feedback through an LED with an auto-sleep function. A dry-herb flower bowl is available separately, but it's primarily a concentrate device. It suits a patient prescribed hash or extracts who wants something portable and modular rather than a fixed rig. Recharge takes around 90 minutes, so it's a sit-down session device more than an all-day carry.

smono-4-pro.summary: An affordable conduction dry-herb vaporiser with a glass mouthpiece and large battery.
smono-4-pro.description: The Smono 4 Pro is a straightforward conduction dry-herb device with adjustable temperature from 160–221°C and a fast heat-up of around 20 seconds. A 3000mAh battery gives plenty of runtime, and the glass mouthpiece keeps draws smooth and flavour clean — handle it with care, as glass can break. The discreet aluminium body slips into a pocket easily. It suits a patient who wants a dependable, no-frills vaporiser at a low price. As a conduction device it's best loaded and used in one go rather than left half-finished.

smono-4-vaporizer.summary: A budget convection vaporiser for dry herb and concentrates, with eight preset temperatures.
smono-4-vaporizer.description: The Smono 4 uses convection heating and offers eight presets from 160°C up to 230°C, so you can step the temperature to suit flavour or density. It handles both dry herb and concentrates, has adjustable airflow, and gets 8–10 sessions from a 2300mAh battery. The full accessory kit, including cleaning tools and a USB cable, comes in the box, which is more than many cheap devices include. It suits a patient new to vaporising, or anyone wanting a reliable, inexpensive backup. At this price the build is basic, but the convection heating and preset range give it more flexibility than the cost suggests.

smono-5-vaporizer.summary: A true-convection dry-herb vaporiser with a colour display, for patients who want even, flavour-led extraction.
smono-5-vaporizer.description: The Smono 5 is a true-convection device, heating with hot air rather than direct chamber contact for smoother, more flavourful vapour and more consistent results across a session. A full-colour display gives animated feedback on heating status, temperature and session activity, making operation clear at a glance. It runs on a Samsung battery, heats up in under 30 seconds, and has an accessible, removable chamber that's easy to clean. The discreet black finish and glass mouthpiece keep it understated. A 10-year manufacturer warranty backs the build, which makes it a sensible long-term choice for a patient who medicates regularly.

smono-70s-vaporizer.summary: A compact OLED-screen dry-herb vaporiser with a retro design, for discreet portable use.
smono-70s-vaporizer.description: The Smono 70's is a compact portable vaporiser with an old-school OLED screen that shows battery life and temperature so you always know where you stand. The chunky, retro-inspired design is stylish but still small enough to be discreet on the go. USB-C charging and a simple kit — cleaning brush, filling tool and spare screens — come in the box. It suits a patient who wants a straightforward, pocketable device with clear readouts and no app to learn. A 2-year warranty applies, extendable by a year if you register within 90 days.

smono-baloo.summary: A budget dry-herb vaporiser with a swappable 18650 battery and adjustable airflow.
smono-baloo.description: The Smono Baloo is a simple, low-maintenance dry-herb device with accurate temperature control from 100–240°C and adjustable airflow to tune each draw. Its standout feature is the replaceable 18650 battery (3400mAh), so you can carry a spare and keep going rather than wait for a charge. Expect 4–6 sessions per cell, with a silicone mouthpiece and a generous accessory kit in the box. It suits a budget-conscious patient who wants decent vapour and the practicality of swappable cells. The plastic build keeps the price down, so it's a value option rather than a long-haul premium device.

smono-lunari.summary: A true-convection hybrid vaporiser with a 360° ceramic mouthpiece and removable battery, for daily flavour-led use.
smono-lunari.description: The Smono Lunari uses true convection for clean, flavour-rich vapour and works with both dry herb and concentrates, giving flexibility in one device. A magnetic 360° ceramic mouthpiece, precise digital control from 100–220°C, and an OLED showing both target and real-time temperature make it easy to dial in. The removable 18650 battery (3200mAh) means quick swaps to keep sessions going. It suits a patient who wants convection performance and good materials without a high-end price. It's built around individual sessions, so anyone wanting a desktop or group-use device should look elsewhere.

smono-no-3-vaporizer.summary: One of the smallest convection vaporisers around, for patients who want discretion at a low price.
smono-no-3-vaporizer.description: The Smono 3 is a very compact closed-convection device that punches above its size, delivering clean vapour through a stainless steel chamber and glass mouthpiece. It handles both herbs and concentrates, with an included oil/wax chamber and adjustable temperature, heating in around 35 seconds. At roughly 8cm it's genuinely pocket-sized and discreet. It suits a patient who prioritises portability and a low price without giving up convection flavour. The plastic body keeps it light and cheap, which is the trade-off for the size and cost.

smono-sunshine.summary: A versatile ceramic-heated vaporiser for dry herb and concentrates, with an ergonomic mouthpiece.
smono-sunshine.description: The Smono Sunshine uses quick ceramic heaters with adjustable temperature control, working with both dry herb and concentrates via the included wax bowl. The ergonomic mouthpiece and sleek shape make for comfortable, flavourful sessions. USB-C charging and a full cleaning kit — brush, alcohol pads and swabs — come in the box. It suits a patient who wants one device that covers both flower and extracts without spending much. A 2-year manufacturer warranty applies from the day of purchase.

storz-bickel-mighty-plus-vaporizer.summary: The Storz & Bickel Mighty+, a hybrid portable dry-herb vaporiser for patients who medicate daily.
storz-bickel-mighty-plus-vaporizer.description: The Mighty+ builds on the original Mighty with hybrid convection and conduction heating, delivering consistent, clean vapour from a ceramic-coated chamber. It heats in around 60 seconds, charges over USB-C with SuperCharge and pass-through use, and runs from 40–210°C for both dry herbs and concentrates. German-engineered with the reliability that made Storz & Bickel a fixture for medical patients, it's backed by a 3-year warranty after registration. It suits a patient who medicates several times a day and wants a device that pays back over years. It's larger and pricier than pocket vapes, so it's a daily-driver rather than a discreet carry.

stundenglass-x-modul-dok-deluxe-travel-s.summary: A complete portable system for dry herb and concentrates, with the Modül heater, Dok glass and travel case.
stundenglass-x-modul-dok-deluxe-travel-s.description: This Stündenglass set pairs the Modül electronic heating unit with interchangeable dry-herb and quartz concentrate tanks, plus the Dok borosilicate glass attachment for smoother, cooler draws. An adjustable carb cap helps manage airflow, and the whole kit ships in a protective travel case with dedicated storage. Heat-up runs roughly 15–45 seconds depending on tank and setting. It suits a patient who uses both flower and extracts and wants one design-led, portable system rather than separate devices. It's a high-end bundle, so it makes most sense for someone who'll use both tanks rather than just one.

veazy-vaporizer.summary: Storz & Bickel's most compact hybrid vaporiser, for patients who want German build in a pocket size.
veazy-vaporizer.description: The Veazy is Storz & Bickel's lightest device at 134g, using a compact hybrid mini-heater that blends convection and conduction for smooth draws without combustion. Single-button operation and three preset temperatures keep it simple, with app support for finer control and a range from 40–210°C plus boost options. It heats in around 45 seconds from a ceramic-coated chamber, and comes in four finishes. It suits a patient who wants the reliability of the Mighty+ name in a far more portable, pocketable form. Three presets mean less granular control than dialled devices, with the app there if you want more.
